description: A protein that is a translation product of the human CD4 gene or a 1:1 ortholog thereof. CD4 is an accessory protein for MHC class-II antigen/T-cell receptor interaction. It is the primary receptor for HIV-1. CD4 has four immunoglobulin-like domains in its extracellular region that share the same structure, but can differ in sequence.
docs.label: CD4 molecule

description: A CD4 molecule that is a translation product of some mRNA whose exon structure and start site selection renders it capable of giving rise to a protein with the amino acid sequence represented by UniProtKB:P01730-1 or a 1:1 ortholog thereof.
docs.label: CD4 molecule isoform 1

description: A CD48 molecule that is a translation product of some mRNA whose exon structure and start site selection renders it capable of giving rise to a protein with the amino acid sequence represented by UniProtKB:P18181-1 or a 1:1 ortholog thereof.
docs.label: CD48 molecule isoform 1

description: A CD47 molecule that is a translation product of some mRNA whose exon structure and start site selection renders it capable of giving rise to a protein with the amino acid sequence represented by UniProtKB:Q61735-1 or a 1:1 ortholog thereof.
docs.label: CD47 molecule isoform 1

description: A CD48 molecule that has been processed by proteolytic cleavage.
docs.label: CD48 molecule proteolytic cleavage product

description: A CD4 molecule isoform 1 that includes at least one phosphorylated residue.
docs.label: CD4 molecule isoform 1 phosphorylated form

description: A CD4 molecule isoform 1 phosphorylated form that is phosphorylated on Tyr residues yielding the active form.
docs.label: CD4 molecule isoform 1 phosphorylated 1

description: A CD4 molecule that has had the signal peptide removed.
docs.label: CD4 molecule, signal peptide removed form
